name: Top Spin
preceding_figure_names: Basic Weave, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Feather Step, Hover Feather, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Weave, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Reverse Turn, Closed Telemark, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Reverse Wave, Three Step, Change Of Direction, Hover Telemark, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending
notes: On step 1 the footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. When this footwork is used the rise will be "Up NFR" on step 2. On step 6 of the preceding Reverse Turn with Feather Ending, Man's body will be facing DW and the Lady will be backing DW. Silver and Gold: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish may be danced turning 1/2 on step 1-3 to back against LOD and 3/8 on Feather Finish to face DC against LOD. Continue with Top Spin to face DW. Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel may be danced, turning only 1/8 to R for Man, 3/8 for Lady on step 7 to follow with Feather Ending DC against LOD into Top Spin.

steps.action: LF back in CBMP, Backing Against LOD
comments: Start turning left hip to left. This is not a large step. Then use CBM.

steps.action: RF back, Backing DW Against LOD
comments: Connect right shoulder down to right side.

steps.action: LF to side and slightly forward, Pointing DC of new LOD
RF fwd in CBMP, OP, Facing DC
comments: See Note for other alignments. Could finish DW of new LOD.

steps.timing: 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1
steps.action: RF fwd in CBMP OP, Facing Against LOD
comments: When Man cut short Feather Finish, Lady respond to keep weight on T (at end of Feather Finish).

steps.action: LF fwd, Facing DW Against LOD
comments: Monitor through Man's central line, keep arm light.
Connect head, shoulder hip to left foot.

steps.action: RF to side, Backing new LOD
comments: Allow feet to swivel, feet turns more than body.

steps.action: LF back in CBMP, Backing DC
comments: Think about stretching diagonally to the right, rather than rotation in upper body.
